output:
**[REASONING CONTEXT]**
The topology flags non-linear rate acceleration: the depletion rate *changes* at each threshold crossing, so I must not project linearly. I must simulate phase by phase, tracking the acceleration as the aquifer drops through each band.
**[END REASONING CONTEXT]**

---

## Aquifer Simulation

**Net draw per year by level band:**
| Level band | Recharge | Withdrawal | Net |
|---|---|---|---|
| > 600,000 | 60,000 | 100,000 | **−40,000/yr** |
| 400k–600k | 40,000 | 100,000 | **−60,000/yr** |
| < 400,000 | 20,000 | 100,000 | **−80,000/yr** |

Note the acceleration: each threshold crossed increases the annual loss.

**Year-by-year simulation** (recharge applied to *start-of-year* level, then withdrawals subtracted):

| Year | Start | Recharge | End |
|------|-------|----------|-----|
| 1–10 | 1,000,000 → … | 60,000/yr | 960k → **600,000** |
| 11 | 600,000 | 40,000 | 540,000 |
| 12 | 540,000 | 40,000 | 480,000 |
| 13 | 480,000 | 40,000 | 420,000 |
| 14 | 420,000 | 40,000 | 360,000 |
| 15 | 360,000 | 20,000 | 280,000 |
| 16 | 280,000 | 20,000 | **200,000** ✓ |

**The aquifer first reaches 200,000 m³ at the end of Year 16.**

---

**Key non-linearity:** Once the level crosses 600k (end of year 10), the deficit jumps from −40k to −60k/yr. Then crossing 400k (end of year 14) accelerates it again to −80k/yr. A naive linear projection from the initial rate would have estimated ~20 years — the threshold-driven acceleration cuts that to 16.
